comment: For an "elite" gaming mouse with impressive features and a price to match, the quality of Razer is absolute trash. I don't know anyone who buys high-end mouses with the expectation of them being disposable in 6 months. I looked at the bottom of the mouse and it says its made in China, go figure. The limited warranty is for 2 years, so this mouse is now going through the RMA process. But after searching online about this problem, I'm not hopeful that the mouse will not return to this state after another 6 months. It seems that Razer mouses have been having this scroll-wheel-failure issue for years.Now I know what mouse brand I'm no longer buying. If you want a mouse that will stay working, looking elsewhere!UPDATE:My Razer Deathadder Elite's failed scroll wheel was determined irrepairable and required a replacement, but this mouse is now out of stock (probably discontinued due to its failures). I was offered the newer Deathadder to replace it, and so I'm now quite happy with the superior mouse. What I appreciate most bout the Deathadder is its incredible ergonomics, among many other well-designed features. So I've revised my rating from 1 star to 4 stars, because of the replacement and the fact that it is a top-of-the-line gaming mouse--if it doesn't fail. I wouldn't give it five stars just because the previous model died within six months, and the mandatory bloatware is not appreciated for a mouse.

Every time my computer starts or restarts Synapse 3 requests 'which account' to use and requires sign in to use settings. A password, name, address, phone number are requested to get Razer ID to use the software. They record FPS and other data which supposedly you can view. All this to "improve your gaming experience". Apparently all settings are cloud based. Razer says mouse settings should work off line. If it's a setting i haven't found it.This is not a new issue. Ars Technica wrote in 2012 “Why the hell does this mouse need to connect to the Internet?” Read what info Razer/Synapse 3 collect. If you don't care ------.  If you clear browsing history, and cookies Synapse 3 requires sign or the mouse is basically a regular mouse.I will use it without Synapse 3. I have had a UtechSmart-Venus for over a year that, after setup, has never lost a setting and requires no Utech ID.Edit: LMB  sometimes fails . I returned it. Caveat emptor

The unit is just built cheap. Not the quality product you expect from a major brand name like Razor. This entry level into the low end market sacrifices quality in too many places ranging from the feel of the plasticky mouse, its weight, and material used on the foot of the mouse. It did come with a nice USB cord, but that was about it.The reason for the two star review is the scratchy noise coming from the pads on the product which forced me to stop using. A mouse, whether or not it's expensive or in-expensive, need feet that glide smoothly on a mousing surface. This mouse consistently made a noticeable grinding noise across a hard surface mouse pad (steelseries gaming mouse pad), where other mice do not have any issues. I attribute this to part of their material selection and cheaper build which resulted in a poor user experience.I guess I bought it to "try" a Razor mouse. Shape was actually good, and I'm sure the sensor electronics are sufficient, but build quality was sacrificed to the point that it made it annoying to use.Also this review asks me to rate "battery life" on a corded mouse... so... 1 star on this rating = NO battery life.

O.K., Im going to "throw" this to the air. Nobody at Razer is listening mac users? I bought this Deathadder thinking how will improve and nothing. absolutely nothing. It doesnt do anything. Doesn't support through internet. I Downloaded  the synapse two and just found out it doesn't work with this mouse, so is like nothing. The worse part is doesnt even work properly without the software, slow  track, have to extend the wrist to move it to the screen at max scrolling speed AND the OS forgets the assigned speed once you close the tab. Honestly, is my mistake, didn't read but magic about the brand and went without asking about mac compatibility (and there is a very disappointed community here). Make me look other options to run an egpu (wanted to buy the chroma but hey... Giving this silent feedback just for a mouse).  NO SIRVE PARA MAC!  If somebody know how to bypass this to configure the mouse, let me know... Because not even using usb override it recognizes it.

The mouse left click started to break within the first 4 months but as of today I can't drag windows from one screen to another. It just stops acknowledging of the mouse click and being held. It feels fine in the hand which is the only reason why I gave it a chance but for a mouse to be unusable less than a year of use is not something should be overlooked. I don't play shooting games, mostly mmos and rpgs. I don't play games that requires a lot of clicks to work but yet this mouse has broken down like I have. I have not dropped it or thrown it. I'm going to see what can be done with the limited warranty after I buy a new mouse but just beware as you buy this.

Last week the middle mouse button on my Corsair M65 finally gave out after months of poor functionality, and I was compelled to buy a new mouse.  On Amazon, I looked at reviews for a Logitech Mouse, a similar Corsair, and the Death Adder.  At the time, I liked my Corsair very much and probably would have bought another one if it hadn't been for a few reviews stating that the middle mouse button was effectively prone to failure.  So I went with the Death Adder despite some otherwise glowing reviews of the Corsair, and a higher price tag.The first thing I noticed about the Death Adder was how light it is.  The box itself felt feather light.  The next, how large; it really is a big mouse.  The cord, too, is light but supple, an improvement over the thicker, stiffer Corsair's.  When I plugged it in, the Razer logo was set to "breathing."  After downloading Synapse, the Razer software app, I changed the lighting to "static" and lowered the brightness to its dimmest setting.  My Death Adder now emits a dim green glow that is not over powering.  The Corsair's lighting effects were so aggressive I'd turned them off long ago.After getting into a game to check out the mouse feel, I realized I didn't like the default lack of acceleration.  Many gamers think acceleration is entirely bad, but in my experience a touch of acceleration is a good thing (for an example of awful acceleration software, use a mouse on a Macintosh computer).  So I went into Synapse and landed on a DPI of 1800, with acceleration at "2" out of 10.  With those settings I was very happy with the mouse performance, and am using it like that a few days later without complaint.After I'd calibrated the mouse to my liking I decided to plug in the Corsair to see how they compared.  At the time, I expected no big difference.  I thought, if someone were to ask me about which mouse to buy, I wouldn't have a strong opinion.  A mouse is a mouse.  The Corsair is heavier, so if you want a heavy mouse, avoid the lighter Death Adder....  Wow, was I wrong.  Even after trying to tweak the Corsair settings (which were less robust, with seemingly no way to adjust acceleration), I could not get it to feel as zippy and responsive as the Death Adder.  The heaviness of the mouse was oppressive, whereas the lightness of the Death Adder made it slightly addictive and fun to use.  Now, I find myself holding the Death Adder and mousing around for no reason but the pleasure of it.The only thing I'm on the fence about with this mouse is the size.  It really is a big mouse, with a pronounced arch.  I think I've gotten used to it, though.  This is coming from a really big guy who can almost palm a basketball.  So if you have small hands you may not like this mouse, though it most certainly is worth giving a shot.  Another thing I'd like to touch on is the aesthetics.  I think this is a really cool looking mouse, though some may find it tacky.  It's a fun gaming mouse design with a really neat logo that glows dim green (as per my setting).  I find myself glancing over at it a lot, and I think it's cool.One thing this experience has reminded me of is how important it is to invest in quality products.  I got into a game of Borderlands 2 the other day, and I couldn't believe how great it felt.  It's because of the mouse.  Don't cheat yourself by using cheap stuff if you can afford otherwise.

This mouse is poorly designed and terrible for gaming. Under the mouse wheel, there are 2 buttons that let you toggle your mousespeed up and down. Unfortunately, during an intense online video game match (DOTA, Overwatch, Counter-Strike, hell even freakin' Hearthstone), those 2 tiny buttons right under the mouse wheel WILL get accidentally pushed. The slightest touch triggers them, sending your cursor all over the place and often to an untimely death.The Chroma doesn't have this issue, because it doesn't have those 2 useless buttons under the wheel. Skip The Elite, get the Chroma

I bought this to replace my very old Death Adder that I've had for I think 15 years now.  The only thing wrong with the old one was that the USB connector had become a little touchy over time and would have to be frequently re-seated into the USB port.The Death Adder is VERY comfortable for me to grip.  I have a "nicer model" (Mamba I think) that is just awkward to hold.  The Death Adder is something that I can use comfortably for hours and the programmable buttons are very handy for use with work (such as copy / paste).  I'm very happy that my previous mouse held up for so many years.  It shows very good quality and I hope that this new one does too.The tracking is very customized allowing you to configure it for various surface /pore density and response speed.The ONLY thing I do not like about the Razer mice is not being able to have a dynamic wheel scrolling speed - where the faster you spin the wheel, the greater quantity of lines per "tick" that the input recognizes.  This particular setting must be controlled through the Windows mouse settings and is a hard set value of quantity of lines per tick rather than being a variably adjustable value.  Maybe this is a fault in Windows, maybe it is a fault in the Razer Synapse software.

Useless product as to use their settings app you have to agree to logging of your every mouse click and keystroke (!), AND SEND THIS INFORMATION TO THEM.Not buying from this ridiculous company anymore.

Everyone knows the Razer Deathadder. It's a chonky boi that fits my large hands and gives me comfort rather than those baby mice of other companies. Nevertheless, they were not without flaws. I am upgrading from a Deathadder Elite after the scroll wheel started to bug out (after 3 years) and it would scroll UP at times when I was scrolling down. The v2 has a number of improvements I think are noticeable.1. The side grips are now integrated into the mouse. This is noticeable because on my previous version, it would peel off. Very annoying.2. The mouse wheel is more tactile and has definite "stops" in its traction. Honestly, I think I prefer the softer feel of the deathadder elite, but this feels more structurally sound.3. RBG is brighter. Yay RBG, go knock yourself out. It's actually too bright for me so I had to turn it down.4. PEG feet on the bottom are sturdier and glide better. Minor improvement, but still felt I had to mention it.5. Overall texture on the mouse is slightly more grippy. Let's see if it holds up to 2+ years of skin oil though.Click action, tracking pristine as always.

I've used a total of four DeathAdder. It's my favorite mouse because it's very comfortable to use. For me with a smaller palm, it can also fit my palm. But Razer's mouse will double-click in three years on average. The first three DeathAdders had double-click problems in my third year of use. My last DeathAdder 2013 I used for nearly six years. Then before Black Friday, it got sick. So I wobbled between ROG and the new version of DeathAdder. The final price convinced me to buy Razer. When I got it, I thought it was lighter than older. It moves more smoothly. The button sounds clear. If Razer can have better quality on the switch, or learn to use ROG without welding, I think I will buy Razer forever.And, I love RGB!When it reached a year, its scroll broke. I contacted Razer and they changed the V2 version for me.

This was suggested to be used with PlayStation. It didn't work. (Shocker) but that isn't why I am deducting stars.The spyware on this mouse is insane, constant pop-ups, and you have to give it way too many permissions. A mouse should not require that much crap. It is ergonomic, and a very attractive mouse, which made the hubby all that more disappointed when it was so intrusive on the computer and wouldn't work as intended. I ended up gifting it to someone who doesn't care about their privacy because it was on sale.

I bought this mouse in April and it’s already triple and double clicking on me. I use this one for work... this model of Razer  mice do not last. I have gone through 5 death adder mice in 5 years. I’m now trying a mamba for home. Let’s see how long this one lasts. These will be my last razer products. I have a key board that still works from them. But let’s be honest... their software is just another forced bloat ware to use all the features of the product. You can use them without installing the software. But at that point, just get a better mouse you don’t have to control.

I've owned mine for under a year and I have noticed over the past two months it is going haywire.First the mouse would subtly register that I was moving the mouse forward on the surface, slowly moving the arrow up on the screen despite holding the mouse completely stationary.Second (and the real doozy) it has stopped registering clicks on left and right mouse buttons. It is infrequent, there is no indication of a pressure issue... it just misses probably one in 5 clicks on the left and one in 20 on the right.Final note, as everyone else has stated the bloatware for this brand is rediculous.Should have trusted my gut originally, going with a better brand.

I got this razer deathadder V2 affer my friend recommended the elite. This mouse if pretty light and feels absolutely fantastic in the hand. For the record, my hand is roughly 7.5cm x 19cm. Great for either palm or claw grip. It glides supper smooth, however a mouse bungee will probably be needed. I got this after trying the ultralight cooler master mm711. the cooler master is light, but feels cheap and not nearly the same quality as the razer. No wonder the shape hasn't changed since 2006! Buttons are firm and responsive. All buttons are easy to click especially when playing fps games. Also this mouse won't slip out of your hand even if you tried. Overall, it is highly recommended. Lastly, the RGB is bright and beautiful.

   Nice mouse so far, I upgraded from a basic dell mouse, so not sure if my comment is worth anything to the true gamers.I definitely recommend getting the large mouse pad also (sold separately), it’s called The “Razer Gigantus large gaming mouse mat” (17.5”x17.5”) Colors change automatically, I didn’t download the razer control drivers for it, so this is what it does straight out of the box (0:53 video I included with this comment)Feels good in my hand, and I like the mouse pointer speed controls on the mouse.I’ll post again for wear and tear update...

I’ve been playing MMOs like World of Warcraft for over a decade but I’ve never invested in a quality gaming mouse before; I’ve only just used the mouse that came with my computer. When I saw this Razer Deathadder Elite on Prime Day for only $35 I thought I’d give it a shot.So far I am glad that I did. I enjoy the feel of the mouse, though it is a bit larger than what I am used to and will take a little time to become comfortable for my use. It feels like a quality mouse made from premium materials, the ever-changing colors around the mouse-wheel and the logo are gorgeous. It does seem to be a bit too sensitive and I may need to dial down the sensitivity myself, but I imagine that people who play games like Call of Duty on their computers would absolutely love the sensitivity and super-fast response.I probably would not spend $70 for a mouse myself, but I wouldn’t judge a hardcore gamer for doing so. But for the Prime Day deal it was a no-brainer.

EDIT: I Love this mouse now! I've gotten used to it naturally over the course of a week or so, and everything seems fine about it. It must have just been a lot bigger than my previous mouse and overwhelmed me a little. I'll leave what I wrote below, because that was my true initial response, but I also just want to say I'm happy I didnt return it too hastily. It fits my hand very nicely now.Also, the colors are beautiful! You can choose from the full RBG, not just a few.Original Post: When I hold the mouse in my normal grip I cannot reach both side buttons. My thumb rests comfortably on the bottom side button, but I simply cant reach the top side button without releasing my grip on the mouse and shifting. I had no idea a mouse fitting in my grip style was a thing, but this is just uncomfortable for me.While the mouse seems perfect, I'm going to to find something else. It's not for me

I've used Death Adder's since I started PC gaming. Each iteration is an improvement from the previous predecessor.  While still a comfortable mouse, it's not the most comfortable to use, but is fine for hours. The LED's are nice, and I like the additional texture added to the scroll wheel. Additional buttons all work well.The only issues I've had with any Death Adder is that the scroll wheel click will sometimes give out entirely, and the left click will also become worn, and not register holding down. But with intense use, this is probably expected, and doesn't occur usually within the first year.

I'll preface this by saying that I am a Razer fanboy.  I have an older Naga that is slowly dying (I play games and work with it) after having it for like 4 years. I picked this to replace it because it has a flatter, more ergonomic grip style where the Naga had a claw like grip that an aging gamer like myself can find uncomfortable after a marathon session. I wish it was a tad wider as my pinkie kinda hangs there with nowhere to go or rest on. Now all that is said, it is scale-able in dpi and has responsive buttons as most Razer products do. Chroma works fine though I don't love the software but that's another review. I would recommend it as a good mid level gaming mouse with decent support.

It would have been perfect if the software worked, I tried everything. I did everything that the Razer support had described, updating windows, turning off the firewall and the antivirus, and installing the latest framework. Without the software, you can’t change the dpi which the default is the lowest. its frustrating when you are in the bios of the motherboard, mouse cursor is SLOW.Update: I accidentally found a way to get the software working, while I was managing the cables in my pc, unplugging and plugging the power switch of the case makes the software appear once again in the desktop if connected the razer mouse. Now I can change the dpi. I’ll still give 3 stars for the inconvenience.... also there is no rgb but I don’t care about that

ive been using the PC since floppy disks were out. i used the microsoft intelli explorer 3.0 then some logitech mouses then i found the death adder. i have had 3 versions of the death adder (green only, RGB, to this RGB) and just got the new version. i love the mouses. they are good quality and do what i need it to do. the new one i just got the buttons in the center adjust sensitivity out of the box. the two buttons next to thumb can go forward and back in the internet - i dont get crazy with mouse buttons. i dont have the software installed and it still cycles through the RGB colors.  the clicks feel nice and smooth the scroll wheel is sweet too. you can tell a lot about a person by how clean their mouse is - lol! i am not getting paid to write this and just with anything in life it can turn out bad or go well for you. i haven't had any of these die on me just kept upgrading and I use one for work too. if you want a mouse in this price range go for it.

Having had two Razer Nagas and a Corsair Naga clone before this I was hesitant.  Noticeably more nimble than the laser mice.  After reading hours on laser versus optic it seems like the latter have made a competitive jump in recent generations.  In games like Overwatch and Destiny this mouse effortlessly glides to the target where the Nagas of the past took more effort to find the target.  I would've given this five stars but the Razer has a newer inferior version of Synapse software it pushes you to download.  After uninstalling I found the original software deeper in their website using Google and that worked beautifully.  I'm still keeping my Naga handy but this is now my first choice mouse.

I resisted Razer products for along time, then used a keyboard.  Logitech's recent quality issues forced me to look for an alternative, and this mouse, quite simply for right-handed, palm user is the best I have found.  Mice I tried that it all best: Logitech G403, G903, G503, G Pro.  The optical button switches in this are just superior to the Logitechs and don't suffer a ghosting problem common in those mice after a period of use.  On top, its the best palm grip I have found to date next to the G403.  I liked this enough I bought one for my desktop and one for my laptop.  Happiness in gaming!

My first razer mouse was a Naga and I'm not skilled enough to be able to use the number pad on the side. But that mouse lasted me for at least six years, maybe a little longer. I got to the point where I wanted an upgrade and I bought a corsair mouse that developed a double click issue. Frustrated I went to a Steel Series mouse, that one I loved but it ended up going the same way the Corsair did. So, I've come home to Razer and while it's only been a short time, I already feel a difference with the sturdiness of the mouse. Hopefully, six months from now I'll still feel this way.

Even though they offer a white mouse, Razer still has not provided software support for Mac computers. You have to connect the mouse to a Windows PC to get it configured. The software, Synapse 3, is the only way to configure the mouse's extra features and buttons. Razer has not ported it. Customer service told me Synapse 3 was to be ported "soon", although every evidence to the contrary. Looking online, I found people have been waiting YEARS now for that port. Physically, it's a great mouse, although without the software I cannot dim the bright light it emits.

As above. In order to shut the lights off this mouse I had to search for and install an earlier version of the Synapse program. The current version continually turns the lights back on no matter what I do.Secondly, after one year the left-click button has stopped working properly when holding it down. When I'm holding left-click down to shoot an automatic weapon at someone in Apex Legends, for example, 4 bullets come out and then the gun suddenly stops firing even though I'm still holding left-click down. So I have to quickly press and hold it down again and maybe it will fire properly or maybe it will only fire 5 or 6 bullets in a burst and suddenly stop again. When I googled this issue guess what other people are having the same problem.

I bought this after my Deathadder V1 died on me after several years of faithful service. The V2 feels just as great as the V1 and still has the same smoothness and responsiveness of the original along with the comfortable feel. the cord is still braided and still looks and feels very high quality. The Razer Synapse software is simple enough to use once you play around with it a bit and it allows you to synchronize with your other RGB Razer gear if you have any. You can also easily change the button mapping using the same software. Highly recommended for not only gaming but for everyday web surfing as well!

I have been using this mouse for over one year and I highly recommend it.  A big part of the recommendation is the fact that the synapse software lets you store various custom game profiles which is very handy.  It also lets you write macros and assign them to buttons.  The one thing that is a negative is the length of the mouse which is longer than my old one and makes hitting the top left most side button uncomfortable for me as I have to stretch out my thumb to do so.  The other negative is that occasionally  ( a couple times a month) the sensitivity jumps crazy high and forces me to jump out of my game and reset the mouse by switching to another game profile and then switching back.  I don't know what causes this.  I also wish it had 1 or 2 more buttons but I am not sure where they would fit to be comfortable as most mice have the same number of buttons or less. So overall, i highly recommend it, just make sure it is comfortable in your hand.

This is a pretty good mouse.I had been using the Deathadder 2013 edition for 6 years until the left click stopped working. During the time i was debating what to buy for a new mouse i had been using a free mouse i got (the msi interceptor). You can't really tell the quality of a mouse until you try something awful like the msi one. I decided to go with the new version of the deathadder, this elite version. realloy is elite. Feels great in your hand, even if your hands easily get sweaty there's no slippage. The scroll is super smooth and clicks like butter. Of course these days everyone color coordinates. I'm happy this also has the RGB function rather than just sticking to green.In short, if you don't want all those fancy macro buttons on a mouse and just want a solid, great gaming mouse. This is it. Period.

   This is an update on my previous review. Dropped it to 1 star. I barely used it but now it developed an annoying noise coming from the wheel when I turn it.  This mouse is cheaply constructed, worse than some of my 5-10 year old mouse and absolutely not worth the premium money they are asking for it. Also slippery to hold for anyone who uses claw or fingertip mouse use. Avoid it.

I'm coming from a Logitech G903, which was a great mouse, ergonomic, light, excellent button press,but unfortunately was unusable as every 2-3 months the primary left or right mouse buttons would stick, not click, lose click, etc.  The Razer Deathadder V2 just came today, and I've been playing Escape From Tarkov and COD, and boy Iam 110% satisfied.  There is no way to describe the material that the body of this mouse is made out of, it feels great,has plenty of grip, and the buttons and scroll wheel are a DREAM.  DO NOT HESITATE TO BUY THIS MOUSE!!!

The quality is that you would expect for the a cheap mouse. It is sub-pair in comparison to the mice at 60 dollars, but you get what you pay for. I loved this mouse until I realized what the company was doing. The had recently (1-2 years ago) incorporated a key logger into their software, which is fine for just the mouse. But I had one of their keyboards, and I did not want my passwords in their poorly secured server, so the mouse became as useful as an office mouse. It was not very comfortable after trying other mice and I regret ever considering Razer to be a good company.

The razor synapse software is irredeemably bad. It pops up annoyingly on startup, asks for admin privileges, advertises razor products, forces you to make an account, does not reconnect when signing out of windows. Compare this to my previous mouse software which sat on my taskbar silently, offering literally every feature that this mouse offers with none of the sheer annoyance.

My 12-year-old son loved this mouse, purchased for his birthday, for about a month, and took impeccable care of it. However, after under a month, the sensor broke. We reinstalled the software, troubleshooted for more than an hour, and attempted to reach the customer service line but were sent to a full mailbox with no way to leave a message, and were disconnected multiple times. The product has a two-year warranty so we were hoping to go through the manufacturer, but ended up needing to go through Amazon return process. Very frustrated with the entire experience.

I have owned this mouse for just about 5 months now and have, beginning about a month and a half ago, come to experience the same issue that many other reviewers are reporting - the dreaded "double click". It began occurring intermittently at first and has gotten progressively worse to the point of me leaving this review. Basically when I press down on the left click button my mouse will sometimes treat it as two clicks instead of one. This is frustrating for numerous reasons - right clicking and clicking on "open in new tab" will sometimes open a new tab as well as redirect my current tab due to double click. Clicking over to one tab from another tab will sometimes select then close the tab due to double click. It's really quite frustrating. Not to mention I've had this mouse for just about 5 months.If you are looking for a long term gaming mouse, this isn't it - I would look elsewhere.

I feel like this mouse is made out of air. Its light and cheap feeling. The RGB on the white version is just.. white - no chroma rgb.I was actually surprised buying this from Razer. I'd never bought a product from them before and this will definatly be the last.I kept it for a week then bought a logitech mouse for 30 dollars. That mouse was league above this one.I really see no reason for this mouse to exist, unless you happen to be a sasquatch with the strength of a toddler.Also to note - I feel like I get more privacy using the drivers to my old chineese gaming mouse than this.

My girl friend is very new to the gaming world, and I got this mouse for her due to its good price, brand and looks. She absolutely loves it, and has no problems after 2 months of using it. Its pretty light weight, and the chord is long. My girl friend has a palm grip style for her mouse, and she has told me it was very comfortable. I have a claw grip way of using a mouse, and honestly it feels odd in my hand due to the overall curve of the back of the mouse is pretty flat when comparing to my mouse of choice (Logitech g403 and logitech g602 wireless). Overall, its a great and solid buy that you won't regret.

I had a prior version of the deathadder for many years, and found it to be a great all-around mouse. This new one seems similar in terms of hardware quality, but it seems Razer has made configuration much more difficult. I wasn't able to get razercfg to work on linux, and I hear that the standard solution for Mac/Windows actually _requires_ you to have an internet connection and create an account for your mouse just to customize settings (see https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2012/11/why-the-hell-does-this-mouse-need-to-connect-to-the-internet/). This wouldn't be such a huge issue, except the default light setting cycles through a rainbow of colors and is pretty distracting (my prior mouse was just a solid green, which I never felt the need to change).Overall, the mouse is well designed but the company has made some questionable software decisions. Why does customizing your mouse need to be so complicated?

First the good: This is a mouse that sits well in my hand. It's comfortable and feels natural when gaming. The LED lights with customization options (colors etc) were a fun touch.My main gripe lies in the 'rubber side grips'. After about two months of use, one of the side grips loosened and then completely detached. I was able to re-glue it, but it fell off again a week or so later. About a month after that, the 2nd (opposite side) rubber side grip loosened and then detached. This is unacceptable for something that bills itself as a high quality gaming mouse. These grip panels obviously need to be secured by more than whatever adhesive they're using.Otherwise it seems to be a pretty solid piece of tech.

I needed an ergonomic mouse as my hand was becoming more sensitive while using my smaller mouse.  This is a larger mouse that doesn't require a tight grasp to maneuver.  Very comfortable.  I use it for work.  It has so many bells and whistles, but I use only a smidge of what it can do (sort of like my smart phone)...  I have it programmed for half of its' capable speed and even at that, now when I use my old mouse it feels like I'm pushing it through molasses.  Great mouse, happy with the purchase.

Ive been using the razer lachesis since like 2009 and its been awesome. It finally stopped working and so i got this. Its awesome....when razer synapse works. Its the most frustrating software cause it crashes on computers startup about 50% of the time no matter how many times i reinstall it. Other than the crappy synapse software, the mouse is great. Glides over the mousepad with ease and the clicks feel great.

I upgraded to this mouse after my Razer Deathadder Chroma started having double click issues. I've been using the Deathadder Elite for a few months and two things I noticed are: The sensor is noticeably better than the Chroma, and this mouse is a little bit wider in size than the Chroma so you will have to make more movements to turn which took me a few days to get used to. Overall, I would recommend this mouse to anyone looking to upgrade from a Chroma.

This has to be counterfeit, the mouse broke after 4 months of being used for gaming I refuse to believe razer sold such a poor quality mouse. On top of that it arrived in a refurbished package which the sales page never mentions. I believe that this was an attempt to cut on cost of selling a counterfeit mouse. I don't think I'm ever using amazon for electronics again after this experience.

Overall this is a 4/5 for me. It is sold as advertized - the glow is cool, feel is solid. The only thing i do NOT like are the 2 side buttons for the thumb. I hit them by accident ALL the time.During a game its no biggie, but i am also doing it on webpages, which forces the browser to go back to the previous page. When shopping online that isnt ideal.True - it may be user error. Just an honest opinion :-)

You have to leave their program constantly running in order to control the light effect. Just buy a Steelseries.Much better and it doesn't require their software to be always on. Also, razor scroll button is way too bumpy. its sharp and points out.

I like the extra 2 buttons on top of it.  I dont care about the color (chroma) but its a nice effect.  I havnt done anything with the chroma settings or sync.The buttons and left and right click have a better snap than other razer mice do.  I got this for 40$.  Well worth it.  I'm hoping it stands up better than my other mice; so far it feels like it has a better overall build quality
This is the most comfortable mouse I have ever used. The performance is of top notch. Still, the material quality could be improved, and I look forward to the on-board memory.

Uparaded from a RAT 7 gaming mouse, was hesitant because this isn't weighted, but wowowow. Feels great, moves great. Outstanding DPI, colors are awesome. Razor software allows customized syncing with mouse pads for ultimate response times.Shipping was a nightmare. Took almost 3 weeks to get the damn thing (and I have prime), but I can't knock the product for that, that's on amazon.

This mouse is great and has the perfect feel. Great build quality. The biggest issue is the software. The mouse at its default setting changes colors through the color spectrum. In order to stop this you have to install their software and make an account. Once you choose the color mode you then have to keep the software running. The mouse doesn’t save the setting due to no internal memory. Very frustrating having to give personal information and run software that updates fairly often just to change a mouse setting. I have to assume that the company has opted to not allow you to save the setting without software solely so they can get more email addresses and info from their users. Their software even has a bitcoin miner you can add as an option. This miner gets Razer crypto for their own profits while they give random rewards for your account. This company just wants to force software on its users. How much more blatant can it get.

I’m 5’1” and this mouse broke my world. It made me realize how big other mice actually are. It noticeably improved my coordination in games and even improved my precision when browsing the internet or doing work.

I'm probably going to get one of these for work, too.  The precision is amazing.  The switches are great.  I had no idea how sloppy my previous mouse was.  This is not too much to pay for something you're going to use for years.(I did turn the lights off.  Download their Synapse app to change settings.)

I received a non-authentic mouse that could not be registered.  This was a poor quality knock-off.  I bought this to replace an identical mouse I had used for several years, so I know exactly what is should have been.  It was priced the same as the authentic versions, so I had no clue this was a scam.

There really isn't much to say about this mouse. There's no documentation that tells how many clicks/rotations before it'll die. It has two buttons on the left side for the thumb for browsing, and there is a light emblem on the rear part of the mouse where the palm rests. It's larger than a standard mouse, so my son (who's 11) says it is more comfortable.

I'm not a pro gamer, but when I occasionally play, the USB keeps disconnecting in the most critical moments of the game. I noticed it mostly happens when I connect the mouse through my docking station. But my keyboard, which is connected via the same docking station, shows no connectivity issues.The design is not as comfortable for lefties.The entire app experience is a pain. Not intuitive at all. If it was cheaper, I wouldn't have complained. But for this price, the disconnect issue is unacceptable.

Its got a good feel in your hand and is big enough to support your palm. the click is the same as other razer mice, my only gripe would be the lack of RGB but it was $30 when i got it and it does go green just not other colors.

I used this mouse for gaming on my xbox 1s. I love the mouse and the tracking is great. But the shape of the mouse might not be normal to you at first. But when you  get used to the shape its a really good mouse.

New Razer DeathAdder Essential - I can adjust it by 100 - still little heavy 96g but can modify using Paracard, remove some plastic "wink" you know what I mean.On Sale for 29 bucks, its a bargain right now.My I have the old Razer DeathAdder 2000 - can only be adjusted from 250,  500, 750, 1000 - never can find the right sensitivity at games.

Coming from a razery abysus which i loved, it took me a while to get use to the bigger size of this mouse.That being said - i am not pro at PC gaming so the change came with a grain of salt. Now that i have used it for a while, I can say it's a great and even better mouse. However dropping one star because the Razer Software is aids for windows.

The white with the white LEDs are so amazing. It matches my keyboard. I'm going for a full white setup. You can't go wrong with Razer products. This gaming mouse is very smooth and feels just right. The mouse gets tons of use and it still works great. Best bang for the buck.

Es mi mouse ideal y me funcionó perfecto el primer mes de uso, hasta ahora. La rueda de desplazamiento hace un ruido muy molesto al bajar, es decepcionante. (no me pasó con mi anterior lancead turnament)EDITADOLuego de aplicar fuerza y prácticamente golpear el mouse la rueda dejó de hacer el ruido, por lo que actualmente funciona correctamente.

I very much respected the original Deathadder Chroma and it lasted me a few good years. However, this new version is crap compared to that.Pros- Comfortable mouse- Chroma colors- Side buttonsCons- The glue on the rubber side grip is terrible, it fell off after only a few months.- Sensitivity buttons are useless for serious gaming and despite disabling them in Synapse, they still mess with my sensitivity.- Cord feels flimsyI'm angry that I'll have to send this to Razer so they can evaluate if the defective gluing warrants replacement and leave myself without the ability to use my computer (which I need to work) for a while.

Great mouse; highly recommended for a myriad of applications. Great customization and responsiveness is excellent for gaming. Great feel and control make this useful for all other applications where precision matters.The only negative is that the additional software required for this sometimes is annoying to deal with. However, this is meant to ensure long-term performance of the mouse itself, which is why I'm still giving this product a full 5 stars.

It was excellent .. I had the old version of them for 4 years .. Then you buy this. There is no difficulty to access the buttons, which is relatively medium weight. I noticed a single defect in which after the use of nearly three months sound appears like the sound of the squeak when moving the Scroll wheel I do not know how I can fix it because it's annoying to me
It's a good mouse, and not too expensive.  Definitely can tell the difference from the basic Logitec/Dell optic mice I've been using.  And it's pretty and fun to play with the color programming!

I purchased this roughly 2 years ago, and really like it. It fits my hand very well, and has 4 extra buttons that you can reprogram.After 2 years of owning this (Very heavy usage), it started to occasionally double-click instead of just the single click that I wanted.

Thumb pad has come unglued, and the mouse wheel squeaks after 6 months of use.  It doesn't do all that well on surfaces that aren't mouse pads either.  I mean, not terrible, but on my wood desk there were a few spot that it would derp, a problem I didn't have with my last mouse.  It's a similar design to the Lachesis, which I used back in the day and loved, but it's such a piece of junk in comparison.

This mouse was my first introduction to gaming mice, and I honestly can never look back. This is an extremely reliable mouse that is definitely my favorite out of all the mice I've had. The shape of it is great for my smaller hand size, and I rarely ever hit the side mouse buttons by mistake. The DPI Change on the fly feature, while cool, is unnecessary for a casual gamer like me. I play mainly FPS games, along with RPG games so it's great to have a legitimately reliable mouse.TL;DR Great mouse, do not sleep on it. I've had this mouse for 2 years and at most the side grips are losing suction, so they slip occasionally.
Great mouse! Wired version which works really well. No complaints

Im already received the razer deathadder v2 pro with damaged skates and the another big problem the mouse disconnected the WiFi connection everytime !!And am already contact amazon to help but no any help for meSo its very bad experience razer

It's such a cool looking mouse. I like the changing of the lights. There is a program that you can download for more features to mess around with. It fits nice in my hands, and the mouse response feels so smooth.

I really liked this mouse at the beginning. I purchased it in april of 2019 and as of now (February 2020), it will not work. I have tried multiple computers as well as multiple usb ports and the mouse just does not work anymore. If you want a reliable mouse, do not purchase this one!

The mouse performance is amazing. I moved from a naga epic to this and the sensor in it is great. The grip took a few days to get used to but after some use it is very comfortable.But even though the sensor and the functionality of the mouse is amazing it has an issue that after doing some research is not just common to my mouse. After having it plugged in for a couple of hours the middle mouse scroll wheel rgb light will turn off. The only way to get it to turn back on is by unplugging and re-plugging the mouse. This is an issue that will continueously happen and it is very annoying for a mouse that rgb is such a big part of.This issue is so annoying that I had to dock a couple stars . But if you just want a solid sensor and don't care about the rgb gamer swag, then I couldn't recommend this mouse more.

The Razer mouse causes my i7 Widows 10 PC system to stall on every restart and boot up. Found out afterwards this is a common issue for Window users. I have tried for weeks to get tech help from Razer. Their suggestion was to un-install Synapse and unplug mouse before rebooting/restarting computer each time. Have not heard back from tech support for 2 weeks since last communication.  I can think of a lot of other ways to waste $70.  Back to Logitech.
this is supposed to be a quality gaming mouse however, way too many missed clicks to be a viable gaming option. lightweight cheap feel also. would not recommend!

This is a great mouse that offers a simple, sleek design as opposed to many gaming mice on the market. There isn't a huge difference between this model and the cheaper version, but the little differences are appreciated, like the grip on the scroll-wheel. I definitely recommend this mouse, but also recommend that you get a good warranty if you play a ton of games, as all gaming mice (especially Razer) tend to break-down eventually from heavy use, at-least in my experience.

The shape of the grips are not good if your play style requires you to lift the mouse. The shape of the mouse is too perfect of an ellipse.  I have to position my ring finger in a claw position to secure it when lifting. If the sides chamfered inward, then it would feel more secure in your hand like the Logitech G903. If you play with high sensitivity then this shouldn't be an issue.

If you have super big hands you might enjoy this mouse. However, for someone like myself who has normal sized hands the large dimensions of this mouse makes the shape feel super clunky and overall just uncomfortable. On top of that, the DeathAdder Elite's weight of 105 grams definitely makes it one of the heavier FPS mice which makes it feel ridiculously slow compared to other mice I have used in the past. That being said, I believe shape and weight to be the 2 most important aspects for an FPS mouse and for my personal tastes the DeathAdder Elite fails to check those two boxes.

I like it. It feels better in hand than the elite. I can't say it's completely revolutionary, but it is a nice upgrade to an already epic mouse. The new feet are much smoother than the elite. Movement is more fluid.

Less then a year after i purchased it, this mouse started to disconnect randomly. I use this pc once or twice a week, so it is not due to constant or aggressive use. Also, it never came of my table, fell or anything of this sort, and there is no physical damage. It just stops working occasionally. When it works it works fine and it is a solid and comfortable mouse, but after 20 years of pc use, i never ever had a mouse that lasted less then 3 years or so. 1 year is kinda disappointing.

Context: 5'10 male that usually wears Med/Large glovesThis mouse is beautiful and fits perfectly in my hands. My only complaint is that the mouse only has one RGB lighting (but that's what you get for $30 I guess). Weight is perfect for me because it's light enough to control recoil easily and for everyday use e.g. web browsing.

The mouse would be perfect unless it had one serious drawback.maybe its just me, but I cant really lift the mouse without trying really hard. the right side is slippery as F and tiny rubber handle does not really help.Otherwise mouse is just fine, just make sure you hold this mouse in your hand at least once before you buy it.

I love the Razer DeathAdder's grip and size. I am upgrading from a DeathAdder 2013, and the additional side grips and full RGB customization is a great improvement. The sensor provides an insane amount of precision. The biggest downside of this mouse is the necessity to have the Razer drivers running in the background. This is no problem if you are using Windows or MacOS as Razer provides drivers for these operating systems. If you, like me, are running a Linux based OS, you are out of luck. There are some good open source alternatives, but they are not as good as Razer's driver. What this means is you get no customization of button macros or operations. I wanted to use the top button to switch profiles which is supported by Razer's drivers. The Linux alternative drivers do not provide this functionality.Additionally, the mouse does not have internal flash memory to save the settings when unplugged. This means you need drivers to restore the RGB color and macros you set before.

Bought this for my son for his gaming.  It fits his hand and does everything he wants it to do.  This was an upgrade from a very cool looking, but much cheaper gaming mouse.  He says it is much faster and doesn’t glitch like the cheaper one.  So as long as he’s happy, I’m happy.
It's super light, very quick an nimble, accurate, sweat proof, grip pads, ergonomically comfortable, and easy to clean. Literally the best mouse I've ever owned, and I've tried them all.
Great gaming mouse. It's lighter and feels of a cheaper quality then some logitech's i had but overall no problems with it and it's been working pretty great.

Good mouse with a nice rubberized feel on the sides and wheel and a good ergonomic form. Nice sound from the mechanical switches. It’s a little bit light. LEDs are customizable as are buttons. Great mouse and perfect tracking. I am not a gamer, but rely on accurate tracking. This is my 5th Razer mouse and can't imagine buying any other mouse.So comfortable and absolutely no issues.

I might have to glue it back on my self and i know it wont be the same if i do that.

as good as the first one I bought when it first came out had to replace it with this one though as mine was a first wave gen and so I ended up having some issues after a small amount of time with the left mouse button not registering an actuated click or it would register one click as two weird right?
Good quality. The real deal. Feels great after long term use and is fast/responsive. Love the white and the controllable lights. The program with it installed fast, no problems.
I have used this mouse for years and year, side button finally gave out so i bought this as a replacement. I think this mouse is the standard by which i model every other mouse after.

I've owned a handful of razor products over the years and this is by far the cheapest least impressive one I've had. The construction is very light weight, and yet still a bit cumbersome. Not particularly ergonomic.
Great product, comes with smart tracking, and moves smoothly. Coming from the razer death adder, to the V2 was a huge game changer. definitely worth the price.
This is the most comfortable computer mouse I ever used. It glides on my mousepad with no restriction and the ref lighting can be pretty cool or turned off if wanted.
One of the best computer mouse on the market. Extremely ergonomic fit. Slides smoothly and is built to last

My right button stopped working and I am out of warranty and they told me go get someone to clean it pretty much, we can't help you. I had this product less than 1 year and it's begun to make a horribly annoying high-pitched squeaking noise when you use the scroll wheel. My son absolutely LOVES THIS GAMER MOUSE! So much that his older brother ordered the same one the same day it arrived! I've used the death adder for almost two years. Extremely comfortable in my hand. The customizable buttons work wonderfully. it's ONLY white for the light but you can dim/brighten it as much as you want. it moves smoothly & of course all the buttons work & i love the extra 2 side buttons (super useful). also i had to ask a friend but it uses razor sysnapse for the settings.

First, let me state that I have owned 3 different variations of the Deathadder. I've had the 2013 version, the Elite, and now the Essential. All 3 mouses have developed what many other customers have described as a 'double click', meaning when you click once it registers as a double. No it isn't your firmware. No it isn't dust inside the mouse. It's simply terrible build quality that has degraded over the years and as you can tell from other reviews, most people are avoiding for that reason. The second big one that can be a deal breaker for some is the account registration and mandatory software bloat. You need the software if you want to even think about customizing your mouse in any way.I loved the Deathadder 2013 mouse I had for a long time because it did what it said it would without issue so when it came time to repurchase after two years or so I happily repurchased from Razer. BIG mistake. Two mice later I am staring at 3 mice that are double clicking and the Essentials build quality is laughable.. A standard $20 Logitech mouse feels better built than the Essential and whatever it's made of feels extremely cheap. The Elite was fantastic but overboard for what I needed but at least felt like it was better build quality. Too bad that too developed a double click issue.. that couldn't be fixed.Rambling aside please avoid this mouse like the plague. Do not be lured in by the low price point (this mouse is nearly always $29-35 somewhere online). I paid $50 for my original 2013 happily, then 40 for the Elite, and now 30 for the Essential. Do not be me. Do not buy this mouse and be disappointed every time it decides to unavoidably fail to work properly after 6 months - 1 year which general everyday gaming on an MMO or otherwise. Look at other products and spend your money where it'll matter.

Like most unknowledgeable kids, Razer was my first go-to choice when I was starting out with peripherals. I thought their stuff was the best because of the cool appearances and market hype. However after trying a few products, I will never purchase a Razer peripheral ever again.They have terribly quality control and cut corners with cheap internal components.I went through 3 Deathadders in a single year. All had double-clicking issues very quickly. Twice I just bought a new one, and on the 3rd I tried to get help from Razer. When I contacted support for an RMA, they refused to assist because I did not have a paper Best Buy receipt from 9 months ago. They offered no sympathy or help whatsoever and would rather lose a long-term customer than do anything. I even offered to send in my mouse first and pay for shipping both ways. Would not budge or do a thing. Very unprofessional overall and it seems they don't care too much about customer satisfaction.For this mouse itself, the shape is massive and heavy. Far too big for almost any hand out there. Not many people realize this because they haven't tried actual quality mice (Zowie, Glorious, Logitech). Razer simply does not compare in quality and performance.For reference, all Razer products are pretty poor quality. 3 Deathadders failed. Tried a Mamba tournament edition, the sensor failed and would stop working sporadically within a year. I had two Blackwidow Ultimate keyboards, both had switches fail in under a year. Razer headset band snapped on my headphones from them. RGB Firefly mat stopped lighting up a little over a year. I spent more money replacing stuff prematurely than I should ever have. I wanted to love Razer, but their products are just crap. If you want a good value and don't want products to break, you should try almost any other vendor first before these guys.

The Deathadder is Razer's flagship mouse, and the form factor that comes from the Intellimouse is still one of the best fitting mice in many palms.  I have had both the original Deathadder and the Deathadder Elite.  I have been fortunate to have them last all these years, I have not had the quality control problems endemic in Razer's line with these mice.  This version has a better sensor and is statistically more responsive.  If you are used to the Deathadder Elite, you will have to spend a bit of time getting used to it as you will overcorrect precision movements because the old Elite is not as good.The real downside of Razer is its software, and I am unhappy to report that both the Synapse 3 and firmware updates are just as bad as before.  If you want software that is not problematic, you should not buy Razer at all.  If you are willing to tolerate the software, this mouse works great.  However, the best part about this mouse as opposed to the Elite is that you can save profiles to the mouse.  This means that you can operate the mouse with RGB coloration and effects from a saved profile without using Synapse all the time.  I highly recommend you make a VM for Synapse for configuration, then not use Synapse at all in games due to its resource hogging and interaction issues with some games.The one hardware drawback is that for some reason, there is a regression in the quality of the buttons.  There is much more give in these buttons than the old Elite, and it is very, very noticeable.  Why of all the parts that these seem worse (and this is reflected from other reviews as well) is something questionable considering the price point and the extreme use that most of us have with gaming mice.  Coupled with Razer's quality control issues, this worries me that the first production run may have longer term wear and the warranty is below industry average.I would upgrade from the Elite purely to get out of the Synapse software shackles.  For new users to the Razer brand, there is a good reason why this is the flagship mouse as the quality, the precision is outstanding, and the designs are always gorgeous.  If I were you though and something that Razer veterans make sure to do, I would buy an extended warranty as the probability of RMA's for Razer equipment exceed the industry average by a considerable amount, and the buttons do not inspire longevity.

Though I got the Deathadder ELITE for $20 when it was on a very generous sale - I was expecting full price quality. It's almost as if they sent me a refurbished model because after less than a year of owning the Elite the right clicker only registers 8/10 times I click it. I could have sworn I bought a new mouse. My original $5 mouse I got from the clearance store 2 years ago functions 100% of the time. WHO KNOWS - maybe it's an easy fix but it is clearly a mechanical issue which I find unacceptable.Now the V2 I got to replace my broken Elite... the first one to arrive was defective. The left clicker was clicking twice and was very loose. I swear to God it must have been a used product because it just felt awful. The replacement I received for free does not have the problem confirming that the first one was either defective or used.The reason I'm being so hard on Razer is that I expected WAY better quality control for mice that are $70. But anyways - the chroma is very nice and the mice run very well as it should. 4 stars only because of repeated quality offenses.

The mouse is nice that is relatively strong sensitivity. It is also fairly priced for a razor brand mouse.  It is a comfortable mouse but the packaging is a bit weak. When it arrived. The bottom was a bit smooched and felt like it could be easily open from the bottom.  All the controls are directly on the mouse so you are able to change the sensitivity. The color can be changed on the application. The mouse feels great though. It is pretty form fitting. It is very comfortable in my hands and glides pretty well across the mat that I have. I have tried it on other surfaces and other than glass it works pretty well on those surfaces as well. There are a few programmable buttons and the RGB is illuminates the scrolling wheel and symbol. I usually just stick with green to be honest as it is the razor default color and works well with my keyboard. The RGB lights are not too bright where you would be blinded by your own mouse. The mouse material is pretty nice making it slip resistant. I would recommend if you are going to clean this mouse just use water/mild soap. If you use alcohol you may damage the material. The sensitivity can be adjusted by the center button or by the application. The application does not come with the device and you will need to download the driver for that on their website if you want to create you own settings. It is pretty simple application which is nice. This device does make gaming a bit more fun with the extra buttons that literally at your finger tips. It makes my healing easier on world of warcraft and final fantasy XIV. It also works great for games like dota and heroes of the storm. It also makes my first person shooting easier to change weapons and punch my opponent if that is applicable. Overall I think this mouse is great with a cool looking design. It is easy to use and smooth movements.

Razer's DeathAdder v2 is a a great mouse, whether you are a gamer or not, or even using a PC.  I have the mouse hooked up to my iMac running MAC OS 10.15.3 and this mouse is so much better than Apple's Magic Mouse.The mouse is made completely from plastic, and it feels a bit hollow, but the plastics used are high quality and the mouse feels robust and well made in the hand.  The buttons and the scroll wheel have a pleasant feel when used and click nicely.  The cord is covered in woven fabric, is very pliable, and does not get caught easily.The top of the mouse is finished in a matte plastic, while the sides are finished in a rubberized material with little nubs for extra grip.  The shape of the mouse and its buttons feel excellent under my hand and my fingers naturally fall into place on the main buttons.  The secondary buttons are also easy to identify by feel.  The scroll wheel is very nicely calibrated and it's easy to do a slow controlled scroll or fast and quick scroll.  The mouse tracks very accurately on my mousepad and doesn't jump unexpectedly.Unfortunately MAC OS does not support Razer's software, but I am still able to use most of the mouse's functions.  The side buttons go back / forward on a web browser, the right click works as expected, as does the scroll wheel.  Just the profile button on the bottom and the buttons behind the scroll wheel do not work.  Also, I am not able to change the lighting sequence.  It simply transitions through the entire color rainbow.All in all... a great mouse.

So, I have owned two of these. My first one lasted quite a while, until my g/f managed to roll over the wire with the office chair and ruin it. I liked it enough to buy it a 2nd time, hoping the flaw of the first one was an anomoly, but sadly it was not. In most respects, its a great gaming mouse, I game pretty much daily with it and it has held up well. However the one thing that drives me nuts and it has happened on both of these. On the right hand side, there is a little...textured pad that is just glued on. In both instances of owning one of these mice, it comes off. The surface underneath is not smooth and you constantly get adhesive on your fingers until you take the time to clean it off. Its a relatively small thing, but it is highly annoying thing that goes wrong.

I used the original Deathadder Elite for a while before switching to Roccat.  When the v2 came out, I had high hopes for it, buy really its not much different.  It has a more precise sensor, but I normally run 500 dpi for gaming, so these 20k sensors dont benefit me much.So I began today testing the Razer Basilisk v2, so I installed Synapse software, it detected the mouse, and I configured it to 500 dpi like I wanted.  Then I plugged in the Deathadder v2 and Synapse wouldnt find it.  The mouse worked fine, but I couldnt configure the dpi like I wanted.The solution to this was to go into the windows Device Manager and remove all the mouse and keyboard devices.  In my case I had 2 HID compliant listings in there along with both Razer mice.  After removing all 4 entries and rebooting, Synapse detected my Deathadder and I was able to configure it.I still prefer for Roccat Aimo 202 wireless over either of these Razer mice, but they are nice, and they do fit my large hand well.  They are probably too big for small hands.  The Roccat Kone (white mouse on left in my picture) would be better for small hands.  Its super light.

Razer DeathAdder v2 Gaming Mouse: 20K DPI Optical Sensor - Fastest Gaming Mouse Switch - Chroma RGB Lighting - 8 Programable buttonsThis new version of the Deathadder looks almost Identical to the previous version, what's new is nearly everything you don't see. The only real visible difference is the new Speedflex cable and a slight tweak to the profile on the right side of the mouse. They reduced the aggressive flared right side which I think has improved the mouse's comfort. As for what you don't see, the mouse is now 82 grams which is way lighter than the previous version. The Deatadder V2 also uses the new Razer Focus 20k optical sensor for precision movements. I never really like the Razer software and felt like Razer's Synapse software was such a resource hog. A cool thing about the Deathadder V2 is that it has onboard memory. Now you can install the app. Program all of your mouse and RGB settings for each game. Uninstall Synapse and the mouse remembers all of your settings. The buttons all feel solid and purposeful with zero free movement or rattling. Such a great improvement to an iconic mouse.

I suppose this is on me, but it never occurred to me in 2020 that a hugely popular peripheral wouldn't support OSX. Apple is only a trillion dollar company. How many Apple computers are there in the wild? So, if you're using a Mac the Razer software doesn't see the mouse. You can use it to right and left click but you can't program any of the buttons and the side buttons won't let you page back and forward on web pages. I wasted $100 on this trash. Don't be like me :D

Had a Razer DeathAdder before and it lasted quite a long time before the glue on the rubber thumb and pinky spots let go and the pieces came loose. Bought this one to replace it and generally the feel is much better than previous ones the rubber pieces look like they have been improved upon and have a better integration that I suspect will last much longer, however I have found that the mouse will sometimes not click, or when I click and hold it will sometimes stop the input for a fraction of a second and then continue, this can be very frustrating when you're playing a shooter and the mouse ceases to function.Overall the feel of the mouse is very nice however in the long run I wouldn't recommend it as the clicking issue is pretty bad and really frequent... :(

I bought the Deathadder essential for use with school and productivity with some very occasional gaming. I knew that I really liked the shape and comfort from back in my gaming days so I figured it would be a good mouse for use with my Macbook too. Being the essential model means that it doesn't have the RGB lights (White light on the white mouse). The essential model also doesn't feature the on the fly dpi buttons (I never used them anyways on my old Elite model) or the rubberized side grips, just textured plastic on the sides which in my opinion isn't really that much of a downgrade. It also features a 6400dpi sensor as opposed to the 16,000dpi sensor on the higher models, which is not something that I have noticed or really care about. The cheapest version of the deathadder was the right buy for me and could very well could be for you. Bonus points for the less gamery white design with white LED's.

Bought during sale for 2.8k as my previous death adder 2013 was double registering left click(after 3 years)...Mat finish gives much better comfort and totally in love with the scroll button wheel(different from chroma)Had to disable the 2 dpi switching buttons, as you need to open Razer synapse again to revert back to required dpi.. heavily annoying if clicked by mistake during game, but just one time effort to disable.. still they are very good to have as you can bind them any action..

I was looking forward to using this mouse as I had heard a number of favorable reviews. I can't express how disappointed I was by this product...I thought my hands were relatively big but I have no idea who this is made for because the mouse was HUGE...i normally use a palm style grip and found this mouse awkward to use and the forward side button not as accessible as I had hoped. On top of that the scroll wheel worked maybe one time in every forty which made it useless for navigating webpages. It is possible that there might have been a workaround,  as it felt more like a software issue than the hardware itself, but after 2 hours of playing around this mouse, it ended back in the box and returned to the warehouse...Looks like I'm going to go with Logitech instead.

Not ideal for people with small or average hands the grip is uncomfortable as the shape of the mouse is really weirdly designed. It could be just not a good fit for me but well for others but I find it just a bit too bulky to grip. I don't have super small hands I'm 5'9 and can almost palm a basketball with one hand.

I have been using this month for over a month now, and it works very well! It is very comfortable in the hand, and I can use it for long periods of time with no problems whatsoever. It also seems sturdy and robust, while also being precise and smooth with the on screen clicks and movements. The changing colors are a nice addition! I definitely recommend this mouse!

I bought it to replace my failing 2007 Deathadder. Using Razer Synapse the mouse gets it's driver taken care of automatically and is as easily customizable as the Blackwidow chroma v2 I already owned which made it really easy for me to setup right after unboxing and plugging it. I really like the addition of the two top button and the rubber side grip is very comfy. The chroma lighting is just a topping since you can't do much with only two buttons being backlit but it's still successfully fulfilling it's purpose of making the mouse appealing. It also feels lighter and more ergonomic than my old Deathadder and the braided cable is another nice little touch. Overall a very good upgrade from a standard/classic Deathadder.

Used to like Razer, but no longer. They don't have support for Mac users... new Synapse 3 program is Windows only. But Synapse 2 doesn't detect anything, therefore I'm stuck with a $100 mouse that basically acts like a standard $15 USB mouse. Actually, it's worse because I can't even adjust the settings on Mac OS at all.Also - I've had quite a few of these mice and they all encounter the same problem. Scroll wheel gets dirty and gets jumpy within a year or so. All it needs is a good cleaning, but the design prevents this completely. The only way to take it apart and clean basically requires you to break it in the process. So after a year or so it's basically pooched.I will no longer buy Razer products.

This is my third Razer mouse.  I have an original Naga, which finally died completely after having the infamous double-click for the last couple years (it was my backup).  The Naga Epic, which also has the double-click plague AND its optical sensor randomly cuts in and out now, which prompted me to buy this, the Deathadder V2.I figured with their fancy new optical switches, maybe the quality would have improved...  mouse button 5 is dead after less than 24 hours.  This is the last piece of Razer gear I'll ever buy.  I played MMOs for the most part, so the Naga was a necessity for me.  Well, I don't play MMOs anymore so I won't be using Razer's garbage.  Good riddance.

The problems this mouse will develop in just a few months after purchase does not make it worth the price.Cons:- Most death adders will develop issues within several months making them inoperableFor me, the right-click no longer works properly (not registering several clicks)Pros:- Ergonomic- Smooth (when it works)

I used to have an entry-level razer mouse, which is really lovely and useful. I cannot resist buying another one when it has boxing day deal. It is amazing colorful when it is on. And it is perfect for my daughter to play games on her laptop.

Solid mouse especially when on sale. Got it for 30 and use it for work. Would definitely NOT recommend this if you have small hands or if you use a fingertip style grip. It’s a larger mouse and really is set up for palm or claw grip. With only 2 programable buttons on the thumb side it might not be enough for some gamers.

Attention a bien regarder si les cable est assez long pour vous. Le cable pour connecter la souris a l'ordinateur et vraiment court. Il ne fait meme pas la moitié de la longueur de mon ancienne G403 de Logitech. Voila pourquoi je mets seulement 3 etoiles. Sinon le reste est correct.

I've been using this mouse for about 1 month now. Everything about it is great so far, it is ergonomically good and feels comfortable to use for long gaming sessions and is really easy to move about as the wire doesn't even feel like it is there. The addition of RGB is also very nice and I think compliments the mouse very well overall considering Razer's aesthetic but it makes it easy to blend in with the setup you have in mind. Great mouse.

I've gone through three mice in the past; that would skip across the screen. You could imagine that in an FPS game how this would be a problem. So far haven't seen a reason to dislike this mouse. No DPI clutch, which honestly I prefer, you can set that in a program that you could or could not download for the device. I thought at first download an app for one device was silly, but having the ability to change anything about the mouse is actual a nice touch.
they are designed in such a way that the left click button has to break after a month of use, thereby beginning the hunt for a new mouse. just buy cheaper ones

i haven’t had any issues with the mouse yet and think it’s great quality for the price i got it for (around 30). i wanted a pink mouse but couldn’t find anything i liked within budget so this was a perfect alternative because the white is really nice!

First of all the mouse itself is amazing. Fits my hand perfectly and very good all around.The razer synapse drivers are terrible. They have been causing system crashes roughly 30 mins into gaming, every 2 weeks (after I uninstall nd re-install them). I ran driver verifier and it determined violation on: RzDev_007c.sys .... That's razers software!!They need to fix their software because right now they are essentially recommending you to install malware onto your system to control the options on the mouse. Terrible software on Razers part, please fix it.
